item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose automatic priority for society over individual rights from their first turns through their final turns. Agent A repeatedly frames Indonesian gotong royong as compatible with rights protections, while Agent B repeats a U.S.-style majority-tyranny and abuse-of-power concern in Indonesian. Both allow narrow public-interest limits, but this is present from the start and does not constitute visible movement.
